DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,640.00. THE MOTION IS DENIED. NO SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY/DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO TOMCOHEN@IONIX.NET. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC AND A REQUEST FOR SANCTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED ANY EVIDENCE THAT RESPONDING TO THESE TWO INTERROGATORIES IS BURDENSOME AND OPPRESSIVE. THE INTERROGATORIES SEEM REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. IT IS UP TO THE TRIAL COURT TO DETERMINE IF SUCH EVIDENCE IS ULTIMATELY ADMISSIBLE. DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITH 30 DAYS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,765 PLUS $60 COSTS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' MOTION FOR TERMINATING, ISSUE, AND/OR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AND AN ORDER TO COMPEL DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC TO COMPLY WITH THE COURTS DISCOVERY ORDER IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC SHALL COMPLY FULLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER ENTERED 12/12/2018, WITHIN 5 DAYS. DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC ALSO SHALL PAY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL LEMON LAW AID, INC., SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500 PLUS $60 FOR COSTS WITHIN 5 DAYS. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And An Order To Compel Defendant Bmw Of North America, Llc To Comply With The Courts Discovery Order
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' Motion For Terminating, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And An Order To Compel Defendant BMW Of North America, LLC To Comply With The Courts Discovery Order

Granted, no opposition filed. Defendant BMW of North America, LLC shall comply fully with the Court's Order entered 12/12/2018, within 5 days. Defendant BMW of North America, LLC also shall pay Plaintiff's Counsel Lemon Law Aid, Inc., sanctions in the amount of $2,500 plus $60 for costs within 5 days.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,640.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua M. Ullman, esq. (626-250-0405) of Lemon Law Aid, Inc. for Plaintiff LEAH RICHARDS via Court Call.
Roger S. Raphael, esq. (415-438-6624) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant BMW of North America LLC.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,640.00.

The Motion is denied. No sanctions.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Special Interrogatory Responses From Defendant Bmw Od North America, Llc And A Request For Sanction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua M. Ullman, esq. (626-250-0405) of Lemon Law Aid, Inc. for Plaintiff LEAH RICHARDS via Court Call.
Roger S. Raphael, esq. (415-438-6624) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant BMW of North America LLC.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' Motion To Compel Further Special Interrogatory Responses From Defendant BMW Of North America, LLC And A Request For Sanction.

The Motion is granted. Defendant has not submitted any evidence that responding to these two interrogatories is burdensome and oppressive. The interrogatories seem reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. It is up to the trial court to determine if such evidence is ultimately admissible. Defendant shall serve code compliant responses with 30 days. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $2,765 plus $60 costs.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
